info.description: Personal search and RAG tool — indexes data from multiple sources and provides unified search.
info.title: Nexus API

post.description: Only available when no users exist. Creates the first user as admin.

put.description: Updates connector config. Masked secret values (****...) are preserved from the existing config.

post.description: Completes Telegram authentication by submitting the code received in the Telegram app.
Initiates the Telegram MTProto auth flow. Sends a code to the user's Telegram app.

get.description: Returns Hidden=true per-message documents for a (source_type, conversation_id) pair sorted by created_at ASC. Supports cursor pagination via `before` and `after` RFC3339 timestamps. Chat-like connectors (Telegram today, WhatsApp / Signal / Matrix in the future) plug in by emitting their per-message canonical docs with matching ConversationID.
get.summary: Paginated chronological browse of a conversation

parameters.description: RFC3339 timestamp — return messages strictly before this time
parameters.name: before

parameters.description: RFC3339 timestamp — return messages strictly after this time
parameters.name: after

get.description: Streams the original bytes of a document. Dispatches to the source connector via the BinaryFetcher capability. Returns 404 if the connector does not support previews or if the user lacks read permission. Use ?download=1 to force an attachment disposition instead of inline rendering.
get.summary: Download or preview a document's binary content

parameters.description: Set to '1' to force attachment disposition
parameters.name: download

get.description: Returns the neighbors of a document across all relation types. `outgoing` edges are the ones declared on the doc itself (attachment_of, reply_to, member_of_thread, member_of_window); `incoming` edges are the reverse lookup — other docs that point at this one. Dangling edges (target not in index) are returned with `document: null`.
get.summary: Resolve typed relations (attachments, replies, threads) for a document

post.description: Recreates the OpenSearch index with the current embedding dimension, clears all sync cursors, and triggers a full sync for all enabled connectors.

get.description: Performs hybrid search (BM25 + vector) if embeddings are enabled, otherwise BM25-only.
Returns current embedding provider configuration. API keys are masked.

put.description: Updates the embedding provider. If provider or model changes, automatically triggers a full re-index. Masked API keys (****...) are preserved.

get.description: Returns current reranking provider configuration. API keys are masked.

put.description: Updates the reranking provider. Masked API keys (****...) are preserved.

delete.description: Deletes every cached blob across all connectors. Admin only. Eager-cached data (Telegram) will be re-populated on next sync only if the upstream media is still available; use with care.

Deletes every cached blob for the connector identified by path ID. Admin only. Safe for lazy-mode connectors — they'll repopulate the cache on next preview. Eager-mode connectors lose cached data that may not be re-fetchable if the upstream source has expired.
delete.summary: Wipe the binary cache for a single connector

get.description: Returns per-source-type/name aggregates of cached binaries (count, total bytes). Admin only.

get.summary: List active and recent sync jobs

post.description: Starts async sync jobs for all enabled connectors. Skips connectors that are already syncing.

delete.description: Clears all sync cursors, forcing a full re-sync on next trigger.

delete.summary: Delete a single connector's sync cursor

post.description: Starts an async sync job. Returns immediately with the job state.
post.summary: Trigger sync for a single connector

get.description: Opens a Server-Sent Events stream that pushes SyncJob updates in real-time. Sends an "event: done" when the job completes.

related_count.description:
RelatedCount is the total number of relations — outgoing (on this doc's
`relations`) plus incoming (other docs referencing this one). The
frontend uses this to hide the "Related" toggle for docs with nothing
to expand, without triggering an extra /related call per hit.

BearerAuth.description: Type "Bearer " followed by the JWT returned from /auth/login.
BearerAuth.name: Authorization
